tasks.title: Add acceptanceCriteria to Task + knowledgeBase to Project (schema + GraphQL)
tasks.description:
Add two new fields to the database and GraphQL layer.

**1. Prisma schema:**

In `apps/api/prisma/schema/task.prisma`, add to the Task model after `instructions`:
```prisma
  acceptanceCriteria String?  @map("acceptance_criteria")
```

In `apps/api/prisma/schema/project.prisma`, add to the Project model after `prompt`:
```prisma
  knowledgeBase String?  @map("knowledge_base")
```

**2. Migration:**
Create `apps/api/prisma/migrations/20260316000000_add_acceptance_criteria_and_knowledge_base/migration.sql`:
```sql
ALTER TABLE "tasks" ADD COLUMN "acceptance_criteria" TEXT;
ALTER TABLE "projects" ADD COLUMN "knowledge_base" TEXT;
```

**3. GraphQL typeDefs:**

In `apps/api/src/graphql/typedefs/task.ts`:
- Add `acceptanceCriteria: String` to the `Task` type
- Add `acceptanceCriteria: String` to the `updateTask` mutation args

In `apps/api/src/graphql/typedefs/project.ts`:
- Add `knowledgeBase: String` to the `Project` type
- Add `knowledgeBase: String` to the `updateProject` mutation args

**4. Task resolver (`apps/api/src/graphql/resolvers/task.ts`):**
In `updateTask`, add `acceptanceCriteria` to the Prisma update data object (same pattern as `description`, `instructions`, etc.).

**5. Project resolver (`apps/api/src/graphql/resolvers/project.ts`):**
In `updateProject`, add `knowledgeBase` to the Prisma update data object. Also add `prompt` while you're there if it's not already updatable.

**Verification:** `npx prisma validate` passes, `pnpm --filter api typecheck` passes.
acceptanceCriteria: Task model has acceptanceCriteria String? field
Project model has knowledgeBase String? field
Migration SQL created
acceptanceCriteria in Task GraphQL type and updateTask args
knowledgeBase in Project GraphQL type and updateProject args
Resolvers persist both fields on update
npx prisma validate passes
pnpm --filter api typecheck passes

tasks.title: Inject project knowledge base into AI prompts + generate acceptance criteria
tasks.description:
Update the AI pipeline to use project knowledge base and produce acceptance criteria.

**1. Zod schemas (`apps/api/src/ai/aiTypes.ts`):**
Add `acceptanceCriteria: z.string().optional().default('')` to `TaskPlanSchema` (after `subtasks`).
Also add it to `SubtaskPlanSchema` if subtasks should have AC too.

**2. Prompt builder (`apps/api/src/ai/promptBuilder.ts`):**

Update these functions to accept `knowledgeBase?: string | null`:
- `buildTaskPlanPrompt` — add param, inject as: `'Project Knowledge Base (use for context):\n<user_input label="knowledge_base">{truncate(kb, 800)}</user_input>'` after project description, before output format. Also update the output format instruction to include `acceptanceCriteria` per task.
- `buildExpandTaskPrompt` — same injection pattern, include AC in subtask format.
- `buildGenerateTaskInstructionsPrompt` — inject KB for additional context.
- `buildGenerateCodePrompt` — inject KB for coding convention awareness.

**3. AI service (`apps/api/src/ai/aiService.ts`):**
Add `knowledgeBase?: string | null` param to:
- `generateTaskPlan(apiKey, title, desc, prompt, context, knowledgeBase?)`
- `expandTask(apiKey, title, desc, projectName, context, knowledgeBase?)`
- `generateTaskInstructions(apiKey, title, desc, projectName, existingTitles, knowledgeBase?)`
- `generateCode(apiKey, title, desc, instructions, projectName, projectDesc, files?, styleGuide?, knowledgeBase?)`
Pass through to respective prompt builders.

**4. AI resolvers (`apps/api/src/graphql/resolvers/ai.ts`):**
Update each resolver that calls the above functions:
- `generateTaskPlan` / `previewTaskPlan`: Already fetches project — add `knowledgeBase` to the select, pass to service.
- `commitTaskPlan`: When creating tasks from AI output, persist `acceptanceCriteria` from the parsed result.
- `expandTask`: Fetch project via task.projectId, get knowledgeBase, pass to service.
- `generateTaskInstructions`: Same — fetch project.knowledgeBase.
- `generateCodeFromTask`: Same — fetch project.knowledgeBase.

**5. Update AI exports (`apps/api/src/ai/index.ts`)** if any new types need exporting.

**Verification:** `pnpm --filter api typecheck` passes.
acceptanceCriteria: TaskPlanSchema includes acceptanceCriteria field
All prompt builders accept and inject knowledgeBase when provided
Knowledge base wrapped in <user_input> tags
Knowledge base truncated to 800 chars
AI resolvers fetch project.knowledgeBase and pass to services
commitTaskPlan persists acceptanceCriteria from AI output
pnpm --filter api typecheck passes

tasks.title: Frontend: acceptance criteria UI + knowledge base editor
tasks.description:
Add UI for acceptance criteria on tasks and project knowledge base management.

**1. Update TASK_FIELDS (`apps/web/src/utils/taskHelpers.ts`):**
Add `acceptanceCriteria` to the TASK_FIELDS string.

**2. Update TaskDetailPanel (`apps/web/src/components/TaskDetailPanel.tsx`):**
Add an 'Acceptance Criteria' section below 'Description':
- Textarea, same styling as the description textarea
- Editable inline, save on blur via `onUpdateTask({ acceptanceCriteria: value })`
- Placeholder: 'Add acceptance criteria...'
- Label: 'Acceptance Criteria' with a checklist-style icon (use ✓ or similar)

Also update the `onUpdateTask` call to pass `acceptanceCriteria` when changed.

**3. Update useProjectData (`apps/web/src/hooks/useProjectData.ts`):**
- Add `knowledgeBase` to the project fetch query fields: `project(projectId: $projectId) { ... knowledgeBase }`
- Add `acceptanceCriteria` to the `updateTask` mutation variables (it's already sent to the GraphQL layer, just needs to be included when calling)
- Ensure `handleUpdateTask` passes through `acceptanceCriteria` in the variables
- Add `handleUpdateProject(updates: { name?, description?, knowledgeBase? })` function that calls the `updateProject` mutation

**4. Create KnowledgeBaseModal (`apps/web/src/components/KnowledgeBaseModal.tsx`):**
- Props: `isOpen: boolean`, `onClose: () => void`, `knowledgeBase: string | null`, `onSave: (kb: string) => void`
- Modal with:
  - Title: 'Project Knowledge Base'
  - Large textarea (min 6 rows)
  - Placeholder: 'Describe tech stack, coding conventions, architecture, etc. This is injected into all AI prompts for this project.'
  - Character count with suggestion: '< 800 chars recommended'
  - Save + Cancel buttons

**5. Wire into ProjectDetail (`apps/web/src/pages/ProjectDetail.tsx`):**
- Import KnowledgeBaseModal
- Add state: `showKnowledgeBase: boolean`
- Add toolbar button (book/brain icon — can use 📚 or create a simple SVG) that opens the modal
- Pass `project.knowledgeBase` and `handleUpdateProject` to modal

**Verification:** `pnpm --filter web typecheck` passes.
acceptanceCriteria: TASK_FIELDS includes acceptanceCriteria
TaskDetailPanel shows acceptance criteria textarea below description
Acceptance criteria saves on blur
Project query fetches knowledgeBase field
KnowledgeBaseModal with textarea and char count
Toolbar button opens knowledge base modal
Save persists via updateProject mutation
pnpm --filter web typecheck passes

tasks.title: AI code review — analyze PR diffs and post review comments
tasks.description:
Add AI-powered code review that fetches PR diffs from GitHub, analyzes them, and returns structured feedback.

**1. Add Zod schema (`apps/api/src/ai/aiTypes.ts`):**
```typescript
export const CodeReviewCommentSchema = z.object({
  file: z.string().describe('file path'),
  line: z.number().optional().describe('line number if applicable'),
  severity: z.enum(['info', 'warning', 'error']).describe('severity level'),
  comment: z.string().describe('review comment')
});
export const CodeReviewSchema = z.object({
  summary: z.string().describe('overall review summary'),
  approved: z.boolean().describe('whether the changes look good overall'),
  comments: z.array(CodeReviewCommentSchema).describe('specific code comments'),
  suggestions: z.array(z.string()).optional().default([]).describe('general improvement suggestions')
});
export type CodeReview = z.infer<typeof CodeReviewSchema>;
```
Add `'reviewCode'` to the `AIFeature` type union.

**2. Add config (`apps/api/src/ai/aiConfig.ts`):**
```typescript
reviewCode: { maxTokens: 4096, cacheTTLMs: 0, retryOnValidationFailure: true }
```

**3. Add prompt builder (`apps/api/src/ai/promptBuilder.ts`):**
```typescript
export function buildCodeReviewPrompt(data: {
  taskTitle: string;
  taskDescription: string;
  taskInstructions?: string;
  acceptanceCriteria?: string;
  diff: string;
  projectName: string;
}): Prompt
```
Prompt: 'Review the following code changes for a task. Check for: bugs, security issues, performance problems, missing error handling, deviation from task requirements. The diff is in unified format.'
Wrap diff in `<user_input>` tags. Truncate diff to 6000 chars.

**4. Add service function (`apps/api/src/ai/aiService.ts`):**
```typescript
export async function reviewCode(apiKey: string, data: { taskTitle, taskDescription, taskInstructions?, acceptanceCriteria?, diff, projectName }): Promise<CodeReview>
```

**5. Add GitHub diff fetcher (`apps/api/src/github/githubPullRequestService.ts`):**
Add function:
```typescript
export async function getPullRequestDiff(installationId: string, owner: string, repo: string, prNumber: number): Promise<string>
```
Use GitHub REST API: `GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/pulls/{prNumber}` with `Accept: application/vnd.github.v3.diff` header.

**6. GraphQL typeDefs (`apps/api/src/graphql/typedefs/ai.ts`):**
```graphql
type CodeReviewComment {
  file: String!
  line: Int
  severity: String!
  comment: String!
}
type CodeReview {
  summary: String!
  approved: Boolean!
  comments: [CodeReviewComment!]!
  suggestions: [String!]!
}
reviewPullRequest(taskId: ID!, prNumber: Int!): CodeReview!
```
Add as a mutation (it triggers AI analysis).

**7. Resolver (`apps/api/src/graphql/resolvers/ai.ts`):**
- Fetch task, project, and PR link
- Get installation from project's githubInstallationId
- Fetch diff via `getPullRequestDiff`
- Call `reviewCode` AI service
- Return structured review

**Verification:** `pnpm --filter api typecheck` passes.
acceptanceCriteria: CodeReviewSchema validates structured review output
getPullRequestDiff fetches unified diff from GitHub
Prompt includes task requirements + acceptance criteria for comparison
reviewPullRequest mutation returns structured review
Diff truncated to 6000 chars to fit context window
pnpm --filter api typecheck passes

tasks.title: Code review feedback loop — AI generates fix commits from review comments
tasks.description:
When a PR has review comments (from AI or human), AI generates fix commits addressing the feedback.

**1. Add Zod schema (`apps/api/src/ai/aiTypes.ts`):**
```typescript
export const ReviewFixSchema = z.object({
  files: z.array(z.object({
    path: z.string(),
    content: z.string(),
    language: z.string(),
    description: z.string()
  })),
  commitMessage: z.string()
});
export type ReviewFix = z.infer<typeof ReviewFixSchema>;
```
Add `'generateReviewFix'` to AIFeature union.

**2. Add config (`apps/api/src/ai/aiConfig.ts`):**
```typescript
generateReviewFix: { maxTokens: 8192, cacheTTLMs: 0, retryOnValidationFailure: true }
```

**3. Add prompt builder (`apps/api/src/ai/promptBuilder.ts`):**
```typescript
export function buildReviewFixPrompt(data: {
  taskTitle: string;
  taskInstructions: string;
  reviewComments: string; // formatted review feedback
  currentFiles: Array<{ path: string; content: string }>;
  projectName: string;
}): Prompt
```
Prompt: 'Fix the code based on the review feedback. Return updated file contents that address each comment. Only include files that need changes.'

**4. Add service (`apps/api/src/ai/aiService.ts`):**
```typescript
export async function generateReviewFix(apiKey: string, data: ReviewFixInput): Promise<ReviewFix>
```

**5. Add GitHub PR comment fetcher (`apps/api/src/github/githubPullRequestService.ts`):**
```typescript
export async function getPullRequestComments(installationId: string, owner: string, repo: string, prNumber: number): Promise<Array<{ path: string; body: string; line?: number }>>
```
Use GitHub REST: `GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/pulls/{prNumber}/comments`.

Also add:
```typescript
export async function getPullRequestFiles(installationId: string, owner: string, repo: string, prNumber: number): Promise<Array<{ path: string; content: string }>>
```
Use GitHub REST: `GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/pulls/{prNumber}/files` + fetch content for each.

**6. GraphQL (`apps/api/src/graphql/typedefs/github.ts`):**
```graphql
generateFixFromReview(taskId: ID!, prNumber: Int!): CodeGeneration!
```
Returns same `CodeGeneration` type (files + summary) — user can then create a follow-up commit.

**7. Resolver (`apps/api/src/graphql/resolvers/github.ts`):**
- Fetch PR comments and current file contents
- Format comments as review feedback string
- Call `generateReviewFix` AI service
- Return generated fix files (user commits via existing createPullRequestFromTask flow or a new commit-to-PR mutation)

**Verification:** `pnpm --filter api typecheck` passes.
acceptanceCriteria: ReviewFixSchema validates fix file output
getPullRequestComments fetches review comments from GitHub
getPullRequestFiles fetches current PR file contents
AI generates updated files addressing review feedback
generateFixFromReview mutation returns CodeGeneration with fix files
pnpm --filter api typecheck passes

tasks.title: AI task decomposition from GitHub issues
tasks.description:
Import a GitHub issue and have AI break it into TaskToad tasks with instructions.

**1. Add Zod schema (`apps/api/src/ai/aiTypes.ts`):**
```typescript
export const IssueDecompositionSchema = z.object({
  tasks: z.array(z.object({
    title: z.string(),
    description: z.string(),
    priority: z.enum(['low', 'medium', 'high', 'critical']),
    estimatedHours: z.number().optional(),
    instructions: z.string().optional(),
    acceptanceCriteria: z.string().optional()
  }))
});
export type IssueDecomposition = z.infer<typeof IssueDecompositionSchema>;
```
Add `'decomposeIssue'` to AIFeature union.

**2. Add config (`apps/api/src/ai/aiConfig.ts`):**
```typescript
decomposeIssue: { maxTokens: 4096, cacheTTLMs: 0, retryOnValidationFailure: true }
```

**3. Add prompt builder (`apps/api/src/ai/promptBuilder.ts`):**
```typescript
export function buildDecomposeIssuePrompt(data: {
  issueTitle: string;
  issueBody: string;
  issueLabels: string[];
  projectName: string;
  projectDescription?: string;
  existingTaskTitles: string[];
}): Prompt
```
Prompt: 'Break this GitHub issue into implementable tasks. Each task should be self-contained and actionable. Do NOT create tasks that duplicate these existing titles: [titles]. Include acceptance criteria for each task.'
Wrap issueBody in `<user_input>` tags, truncate to 3000 chars.

**4. Add service (`apps/api/src/ai/aiService.ts`):**
```typescript
export async function decomposeIssue(apiKey: string, data: DecomposeIssueInput): Promise<IssueDecomposition>
```

**5. Add GitHub issue fetcher to use existing `getGitHubIssue` (`apps/api/src/github/githubIssueService.ts`):**
The existing function returns `{ state, title, labels }` but we also need the body. Update it to return `{ state, title, body, labels }` by adding `body` to the GraphQL query.

**6. GraphQL (`apps/api/src/graphql/typedefs/github.ts`):**
```graphql
decomposeGitHubIssue(projectId: ID!, issueNumber: Int!): [Task!]!
```
Returns created tasks (like commitTaskPlan).

**7. Resolver (`apps/api/src/graphql/resolvers/github.ts`):**
- Fetch project + installation info
- Fetch issue from GitHub via `getGitHubIssue`
- Fetch existing task titles for dedup
- Call `decomposeIssue` AI service
- Create tasks in DB with results (title, description, priority, instructions, acceptanceCriteria)
- Link tasks to the GitHub issue (store issueNumber and nodeId)
- Return created tasks

**Verification:** `pnpm --filter api typecheck` passes.
acceptanceCriteria: IssueDecompositionSchema validates task array output
getGitHubIssue returns issue body alongside title/state/labels
Prompt includes existing task titles for dedup
decomposeGitHubIssue mutation creates tasks in DB
Created tasks linked to GitHub issue (issueNumber + nodeId)
pnpm --filter api typecheck passes

tasks.title: Generated code diff view in CodePreviewModal
tasks.description:
Show a diff view comparing generated code against existing repo files.

**1. Add file content fetcher (`apps/api/src/github/githubFileService.ts`):**
Add function (or verify it exists):
```typescript
export async function fetchFileContent(installationId: string, owner: string, repo: string, path: string, ref?: string): Promise<string | null>
```
Use GitHub REST: `GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/contents/{path}?ref={ref}`. Returns base64-decoded content, or null if file doesn't exist.

**2. GraphQL (`apps/api/src/graphql/typedefs/github.ts`):**
```graphql
fetchRepoFileContent(projectId: ID!, filePath: String!): String
```
Returns file content or null if not found. Query (read-only).

**3. Resolver (`apps/api/src/graphql/resolvers/github.ts`):**
- Fetch project's repo info
- Call `fetchFileContent` with default branch
- Return content string or null

**4. Create diff utility (`apps/web/src/utils/diff.ts`):**
Implement a simple line-based diff (no library):
```typescript
export function computeDiff(oldLines: string[], newLines: string[]): DiffLine[]
export interface DiffLine {
  type: 'added' | 'removed' | 'unchanged';
  content: string;
  oldLineNumber?: number;
  newLineNumber?: number;
}
```
Use a simple LCS (longest common subsequence) approach or Myers diff. Keep it under 100 lines.

**5. Update CodePreviewModal (`apps/web/src/components/CodePreviewModal.tsx`):**
- Add a 'Diff' toggle button next to each file header (only shown when project has a repo)
- When toggled on for a file, fetch the original content via `fetchRepoFileContent`
- Display side-by-side or unified diff view using the diff utility:
  - Red background for removed lines
  - Green background for added lines
  - Gray for unchanged context lines
- Cache fetched file contents in component state to avoid re-fetching
- If file doesn't exist in repo (new file), show 'New file' indicator

**Verification:** `pnpm typecheck` passes for both packages.
acceptanceCriteria: fetchRepoFileContent returns file content from GitHub repo
fetchRepoFileContent query available in GraphQL
diff.ts computes line-based diff between old and new content
CodePreviewModal shows diff toggle per file when repo is connected
Diff view shows added/removed/unchanged lines with color coding
New files show 'New file' indicator instead of diff
pnpm typecheck passes for both packages
